spec.why: In a presentation rehearsal the key moments are the presenter's engaged delivery (leaning in + eye contact with audience), shared audience focus on a referenced object, and any disruption to the group; eye contact with the robot or turn-taking (e.g. handing off the presenter role) are independently newsworthy.

spec.why: In a rehearsal the key moments are the presenter accidentally addressing the robot, the audience jointly attending to a shared visual, and the presenter leaning in to manipulate props/slides — plus arrivals or departures that change the rehearsal group.
spec.missing: floor-hold / speaking-turn — who currently has the presenter role (distinct from artifact turn-taking)

spec.why: In a presentation rehearsal the key moments are the presenter's engaged delivery (leaning in while making eye contact), the audience jointly attending to a shared reference (possibly pointed at), and any turn-taking or formation shift that signals a role handoff or interruption — arrivals/departures also matter as they change the rehearsal dynamic.
